goal: Fix Swiss-cheese instance masks on occluded apples caused by labeled leaves in the instance channel.
thoughts: Instance seg returns topmost LABELED instance per pixel — leaves correctly occlude because they're labeled. Real root causes: leaves tagged as instances when they shouldn't be, apples sharing IDs, or annotator sampling at lower res than RGB. Priya wants amodal but Isaac Sim doesn't ship an amodal annotator. Honest fix: (a) strip leaves from instance channel keeping them semantic, (b) custom annotator for true amodal. Don't promise fill_occlusion_holes=True.
code:
# 1. Audit leaf labels — are leaves in the instance channel at all?
leaves = list_all_prims(type_filter="Xform", name_contains="leaf")
for l in leaves[:5]:
    print(get_semantic_label(prim_path=l.path))
validate_semantic_labels(scene="current")

# 2. Preferred fix: keep leaves in SEMANTIC channel, remove from INSTANCE
# By convention, instance-seg only returns prims with instanceId semantic API.
# Strip instance semantic from leaves; leaves stay class-labeled 'leaf' semantically.
for l in leaves:
    # remove instanceId semantic but keep class 'leaf'
    configure_sdg(
        stage="semantics",
        operation="unset_instance_id",
        prim_path=l.path,
    )

# 3. If Priya truly needs amodal (through-leaf) apple masks
lookup_knowledge(query="replicator custom annotator amodal mask z-order stripping")
# Honest: this requires a custom annotator writing a separate amodal_mask channel,
# generated by ray-testing apple prims ignoring leaf occluders.
create_sdg_pipeline(name="apples_amodal", annotators=["rgb", "semantic", "amodal_custom"])
failure_modes: assistant promises a fill_occlusion_holes writer flag
assistant recommends numpy dilation as primary fix
assistant claims Isaac Sim ships amodal annotator
assistant doesn't explain WHY holes appear (labeled leaf occluders)
